advisory.title: CWA-2024-005: Stackoverflow in wasmd
advisory.description: wasmd advisory CWA-2024-005 covers an uncontrolled-recursion / stack-overflow condition in the x/wasm message dispatch path. Deeply nested submessage chains could exhaust the Go stack and crash the node. The fix introduces a recursion depth limit (max call depth) in the handler plugin and keeper configuration. Affects wasmd >= 0.50.0 < 0.53.0 and < 0.46.0; fixed in 0.53.0 and 0.46.0.

introducingCommits.subject: Submessage reply can overwrite caller response (#502)

timeline.notes: vulnerableHead is the v0.52.0 release commit, the last vulnerable v0.5x release before the v0.53.0 patch. The introducing commit 305f13cc added the recursive submessage dispatcher (MessageDispatcher.DispatchSubmessages in x/wasm/keeper/msg_dispatcher.go) — the call path that re-enters contract execution without a depth cap. Both the v0.46.0 and v0.53.0 patches add a recursion depth limit (callDepthMessageHandler, checkAndIncreaseCallDepth) wrapping this dispatcher in x/wasm/keeper/handler_plugin.go and keeper.go.

expectedOutcome.description: Scanner should flag unbounded recursion in MessageDispatcher.DispatchSubmessages (x/wasm/keeper/msg_dispatcher.go) — the call path that re-enters contract execution without a depth cap — and/or the fix-site files (handler_plugin.go, keeper.go) where the patched releases wrap the dispatcher with callDepthMessageHandler/checkAndIncreaseCallDepth. A finding on any of these three paths counts as path-correct.

checks.name: advisory_published
checks.details: CWA-2024-005 (GHSA-g8w7-7vgg-x7xg) was published on 2024-08-21 with patches in wasmd v0.53.0 and v0.46.0.

checks.name: vulnerable_head_in_advisory_range
checks.details: vulnerableHead is the v0.52.0 release commit, the latest tag in the >=0.50.0,<0.53.0 vulnerable range.
